description: Barrio Azteca is a Mexican-American criminal organization originating in El Paso, Texas, founded in 1986 as a prison gang. It has grown into a multinational criminal enterprise with thousands of members in the US and Mexico, primarily involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other organized crime. The group is allied with the Juárez Cartel and La Línea, and is known for its violent cross-border operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: El líder de Barrio Azteca, Arturo Gallegos Castrellón, más conocido por su apodo El Farmero, ordenó la ejecución de dos trabajadores del consulado estadounidense y un mexicano vinculado a la agencia en Ciudad Juárez.
country_links.context: Barrio Azteca operates in Mexico, carrying out attacks and controlling drug trafficking routes in Ciudad Juárez.
